words.meaning_en: Complaining in a petulant or whining manner
words.example_sentence: The querulous patient complained about every aspect of her hospital stay.

one_word_sub.question: A person who constantly complains in a whining or petty manner

words.sentence: The ______ employee was never satisfied regardless of how much the conditions improved.
words.example_sentence_hi: शिकायती मरीज ने अपने अस्पताल प्रवास के हर पहलू के बारे में शिकायत की।

words.meaning_en: Bitterness or resentfulness, especially when long-standing
words.example_sentence: Years of rancour between the two families made reconciliation impossible.

one_word_sub.question: Deep-seated bitterness and resentment towards someone

words.sentence: The political debate was marred by personal ______ rather than policy discussion.
words.example_sentence_hi: दोनों परिवारों के बीच वर्षों के द्वेष ने सुलह असंभव बना दी।

words.meaning_en: Not revealing one's thoughts or feelings readily
words.example_sentence: The reticent witness had to be coaxed into giving his account of events.

one_word_sub.question: A person who does not readily share thoughts or feelings

analogy.question: reluctant to speak or reveal feelings : ______

words.sentence: The CEO remained ______ about the company's merger plans during the press conference.
words.example_sentence_hi: संकोची गवाह को अपनी घटना की जानकारी देने के लिए मनाना पड़ा।

words.meaning_en: Having or showing keen mental discernment and good judgement
words.example_sentence: The sagacious diplomat resolved the crisis without any bloodshed.

one_word_sub.question: A person who is wise and has great ability to make good decisions

words.sentence: The ______ king was known for settling disputes with fairness and wisdom.
words.example_sentence_hi: बुद्धिमान राजनयिक ने बिना रक्तपात के संकट को सुलझाया।

words.meaning_en: Optimistic, especially in a difficult situation
words.example_sentence: Despite the setback, she remained sanguine about the project's chances.

one_word_sub.question: A person who is optimistic even in difficult circumstances

words.sentence: The finance minister was ______ about achieving the growth target despite global headwinds.
words.example_sentence_hi: असफलता के बावजूद वह परियोजना की संभावनाओं के बारे में आशावादी रही।

words.meaning_en: A person who acts obsequiously towards someone important in order to gain advantage
words.example_sentence: The CEO was surrounded by sycophants who never pointed out his mistakes.

one_word_sub.question: A person who flatters important people to gain personal advantage

analogy.question: one who flatters to gain favour : ______

words.sentence: True advisors give honest feedback; ______s only tell you what you want to hear.
words.example_sentence_hi: सीईओ के आसपास चापलूस थे जो उसकी गलतियाँ कभी नहीं बताते थे।

words.example_sentence: He had the temerity to blame his mentor for his own failures.

one_word_sub.question: Excessive boldness or rashness beyond what is appropriate

words.sentence: The junior officer had the ______ to question the general's strategy in public.
words.example_sentence_hi: उसने अपनी विफलताओं के लिए अपने गुरु को दोष देने का दुस्साहस किया।

words.meaning_en: Lasting only for a short time; impermanent
words.example_sentence: Happiness based on material wealth is often transient.

one_word_sub.question: Something that lasts for a very short time before passing

words.sentence: The pain was ______ and disappeared after two days of rest.
words.example_sentence_hi: भौतिक संपदा पर आधारित खुशी अक्सर क्षणिक होती है।

words.meaning_en: Eager or quick to argue or fight; aggressively defiant
words.example_sentence: The truculent defendant constantly interrupted the judge during proceedings.

one_word_sub.question: A person who is eager to fight or argue aggressively

analogy.question: aggressively defiant and eager to fight : ______

words.sentence: The ______ negotiator's attitude derailed the peace talks.
words.example_sentence_hi: झगड़ालू प्रतिवादी ने सुनवाई के दौरान बार-बार न्यायाधीश को बाधित किया।

words.meaning_en: Characterized by conflict, disorder, or confusion; not calm
words.example_sentence: India went through a turbulent phase during the partition of 1947.

one_word_sub.question: A period or situation full of conflict and disorder

words.sentence: The stock market went through a ______ week after the global economic crisis.
words.example_sentence_hi: 1947 के विभाजन के दौरान भारत एक अशांत दौर से गुजरा।

words.example_sentence: Smartphones have become ubiquitous in both urban and rural India.

one_word_sub.question: Something found or seeming to be everywhere at the same time

words.sentence: The ______ presence of plastic pollution has become a global environmental crisis.
words.example_sentence_hi: शहरी और ग्रामीण दोनों भारत में स्मार्टफोन सर्वव्यापी हो गए हैं।

words.meaning_en: Waver between different opinions or actions; be indecisive
words.example_sentence: The committee vacillated on the issue for months without reaching a decision.

one_word_sub.question: To keep changing one's mind or be unable to decide

words.sentence: A good leader does not ______ when a quick, firm decision is needed.
words.example_sentence_hi: समिति महीनों तक इस मुद्दे पर निर्णय लिए बिना डगमगाती रही।

words.meaning_en: Offering nothing that is stimulating or challenging; bland
words.example_sentence: The film's vapid plot disappointed critics who expected depth.

one_word_sub.question: Something that is dull and lacking in ideas or intelligence

words.sentence: The celebrity's ______ remarks added nothing to the debate on climate change.
words.example_sentence_hi: फिल्म की नीरस कहानी ने उन आलोचकों को निराश किया जो गहराई की उम्मीद कर रहे थे।

words.meaning_en: Regard with great respect and reverence
words.example_sentence: In Indian culture, elders are venerated as sources of wisdom.

one_word_sub.question: To regard someone or something with deep respect and reverence

words.sentence: The memorial was built to ______ those who gave their lives for the nation.
words.example_sentence_hi: भारतीय संस्कृति में बुजुर्गों को ज्ञान के स्रोत के रूप में श्रद्धा दी जाती है।

words.meaning_en: Using or expressed in more words than are needed
words.example_sentence: His verbose writing style made the report unnecessarily long.

one_word_sub.question: A style of speaking or writing that uses too many words

words.sentence: The ______ instructions confused more than they helped.
words.example_sentence_hi: उसकी शब्दाडंबरी लेखन शैली ने रिपोर्ट को अनावश्यक रूप से लंबा बना दिया।

words.meaning_en: Having or showing a strong desire to harm someone who has harmed you
words.example_sentence: The vindictive manager gave the whistleblower a poor performance review.

one_word_sub.question: A person who seeks revenge and wants to harm those who wronged them

words.sentence: His ______ response to criticism destroyed his reputation among peers.
words.example_sentence_hi: प्रतिशोधी प्रबंधक ने व्हिसलब्लोअर को खराब प्रदर्शन समीक्षा दी।

words.meaning_en: Feeling or showing caution about possible dangers or problems
words.example_sentence: Investors remain wary of the volatile cryptocurrency market.

one_word_sub.question: A person who is cautious and watchful for possible danger

words.sentence: Be ______ of investment schemes that promise unrealistic returns.
words.example_sentence_hi: निवेशक अस्थिर क्रिप्टोकरेंसी बाजार के प्रति सतर्क बने हुए हैं।

words.meaning_en: A person who is fanatical and uncompromising in pursuit of their beliefs
words.example_sentence: The religious zealot refused to engage in any rational debate.

one_word_sub.question: A person fanatically devoted to a cause and unwilling to compromise

words.sentence: A ______ cannot be reasoned with because they refuse to consider opposing views.
words.example_sentence_hi: धार्मिक कट्टरपंथी ने किसी तर्कसंगत बहस में भाग लेने से इनकार कर दिया।
